summary: Fixed critical infinite loading bug in AuthContext.js. All 7 requested tests now pass.
passed_tests: Login page loads without infinite loading (loaded in <0.1s, no spinner)
Login form is visible and functional (email, password, submit, Google login all work)
Logo displays correctly without stretching (448x64 pixels)
Theme toggle on login page works (dark/light mode switch)
Register link on login page navigates correctly to /register
Page navigation to /login works and doesn't get stuck in loading state
/dashboard redirects to /login if not authenticated (no infinite loading)
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed AuthContext.js infinite loading bug - the old implementation used useRef to prevent double initialization in StrictMode, but this caused the onAuthStateChange listener to not be properly set up on remounts. The new implementation uses a 'mounted' flag pattern instead and explicitly calls getSession() for initial auth state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: AuthContext.js was fixed to resolve infinite loading on protected routes. The fix uses getSession() for initial auth state plus onAuthStateChange for subsequent changes, with a mounted flag to prevent state updates after unmount. Safety timeout reduced to 3 seconds.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The previous AuthContext implementation used useRef(initialized) to prevent double initialization in React StrictMode. However, StrictMode unmounts and remounts components, which cleared the timeout and unsubscribed the listener, but initialized.current remained true. On remount, the effect returned early due to initialized.current === true, so no auth check was ever performed, leaving loading stuck at true forever.
reproduction_steps: Navigate directly to /dashboard as unauthenticated user
loading state stuck at true, never becoming false
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Replaced useRef guard with mounted flag pattern. Added explicit getSession() call for initial auth state (not relying solely on onAuthStateChange). The mounted flag is properly reset on each effect run, allowing the auth initialization to work correctly with StrictMode.
